ip_outcome_note: Depicted figure is canonically Chinchou (National Pokedex no. 170): round blue body, yellow antennal orbs, aquatic deep-sea habitat, bioluminescent glow used for communication — all confirmed per PokemonTCG.io API card data and Pokedex lore. ip_verified set to true.

trivia_bullets.text: Paldean Fates (set code sv4pt5) is a Scarlet & Violet special subset released 2024-01-26, featuring shiny variants of Generation 9 Paldean Pokemon. All 91 cards in the main set and 154 secret rares display shiny colorations; this is the TCG's second comprehensive shiny-focused special subset. [PokemonTCG.io API: sv4pt5 set data]

Chinchou is National Pokedex no. 170, introduced in Generation 2 (Gold and Silver, 1999). It is a Water/Electric dual-type classified as the Angler Pokemon, designed around the deep-sea anglerfish (specifically the footballfish). Its antennae carry electricity-generating cells that power the bioluminescent orbs it uses both to lure prey and communicate with others of its kind. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia Chinchou via snippet; PokemonTCG.io API: nationalPokedexNumbers]

Flavor text: "On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights." — Directly references the species' lore-established behavior: Chinchou flashes its bioluminescent antennal orbs as its primary mode of communication in lightless deep-sea environments. [PokemonTCG.io API / Scryfall ground-truth: flavor_text field]

Artist credited as yuu (lowercase). The card is a Basic Lightning-type (despite Chinchou's in-game Water/Electric dual typing; TCG typing maps Electric types as Lightning). It evolves into Lanturn (also in the TCG pool). The Paldean Fates printing is Chinchou's shiny-variant TCG appearance, depicting its shiny coloration. [PokemonTCG.io API: artist, types, evolvesTo fields]
